input._comment_file: Path to input file, relative to <workspace>/INPUT/ or absolute.
input._comment_time_format: Options: gpst_seconds | gpst_week_sow | utc | mjd | unix | index
input._comment_time_columns: 0-based field indices that form the time token. Empty = field 0 only. Use [0,1] when date and time are in separate columns, e.g. '1990-01-01' '00:00:00'.
input._comment_delimiter: Field separator. Use ' ' for space-delimited files.
input._comment_comment_char: Lines starting with this character are skipped.
input._comment_columns: 1-based indices of value columns to load. Empty = load all value columns. Index counts from 1 after the time field(s).
input._comment_merge_strategy: Options: separate (each file -> independent series) | merge (all files concatenated and sorted).

plots._comment_time_format: X-axis label format. Options: mjd | utc | gpst_seconds | unix. Empty = inherit from input.time_format.

enabled._comment_generic: Generic plots applied to the original series.
enabled._comment_deseasonalized: Residuals with outlier markers. Only rendered when deseasonalization is active.

stats._comment_hurst: Compute Hurst exponent. Can be slow for large series.

deseasonalization._comment_strategy: Subtract a seasonal component before detection so that detectors operate on residuals rather than the raw signal. Options:
deseasonalization._comment_strategy_median_year: median_year    -- Subtracts median annual profile. Best for climatological and GNSS data with a clear annual cycle.
deseasonalization._comment_strategy_moving_average: moving_average -- Subtracts centred moving average. Best for sensor / signal data without a strict annual period.
deseasonalization._comment_strategy_none: none           -- No deseasonalization. Use when the series has no periodic component (economic, count, residuals).
deseasonalization._comment_ma_window_size: Window length in samples for moving_average. Examples: daily data: 365 = 1 year. 6-hourly data: 1461 = 1 year. 1-second data: 86400 = 1 day.
deseasonalization._comment_median_year_min_years: Minimum number of years of data required per day-of-year slot in the median annual profile. Slots with fewer years produce NaN and are not subtracted.

detection._comment_method_iqr: iqr        -- IQR-based (box-plot rule). Flags values outside Q1/Q3 +/- multiplier * IQR. Classic, widely used.
detection._comment_method_mad: mad        -- MAD-based (robust). Flags values outside median +/- k * MAD / 0.6745. Robust to non-Gaussian tails.
detection._comment_method_zscore: zscore     -- Z-score. Flags values with |z| > threshold. Assumes approximate normality of residuals.
detection._comment_method_mad_bounds: mad_bounds -- Global MAD bounds on the raw series. No deseasonalization required. Best for coarse removal of physically impossible values (e.g. sensor overflow, instrument resets).
detection._comment_iqr_multiplier: Fence width multiplier for iqr method. Standard box-plot rule: 1.5 (mild), 3.0 (extreme). Default: 1.5.
detection._comment_mad_multiplier: Threshold multiplier for mad and mad_bounds methods. Roughly analogous to sigma units after normalisation by 0.6745. Default: 3.0. Use 5.0 or higher for coarse pre-filtering.
detection._comment_zscore_threshold: Z-score threshold. Values with |z| > threshold are flagged. Default: 3.0 (corresponds to ~0.3 % of Gaussian tail).

replacement._comment_strategy: How to fill NaN values left after outlier removal. Options:
replacement._comment_strategy_linear: linear       -- Linear interpolation between the nearest valid neighbours. Best for smooth signals.
replacement._comment_strategy_forward_fill: forward_fill -- Copy the last valid value forward. Best for step-like or slowly varying signals.
replacement._comment_strategy_mean: mean         -- Replace with the series mean. Use only when temporal order is not meaningful.
replacement._comment_max_fill_length: Maximum number of consecutive NaN samples to fill. 0 = no limit. Set > 0 to leave long gaps as NaN (e.g. instrument outages that should not be interpolated).
